fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Ohio Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity ohio, how to transfer pharmacist license to ohio, pharmacist compact ohio, ohio pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Ohio Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 77 S. High St., 17th Floor, Columbus, OH 43215-6126

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 40 hours of approved CE every two years

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Ohio.

steps.title: Create Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the Ohio eLicense Portal (elicense.ohio.gov) and complete the Pharmacist Licensure Application, selecting the appropriate application type (e.g., initial or endorsement).

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents, such as proof of identity, pharmacy education verification, and other required forms, directly within your application on the Ohio eLicense Portal.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Records Checks
steps.description: Arrange for both an Ohio Bureau of Criminal Identification and Investigation (BCI) check and an FBI federal criminal background check, ensuring the results are sent directly to the Ohio Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pass Licensure Examinations
steps.description: For initial licensure, register for and pass both the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX) and the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE), with scores reported directly to the Ohio Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Ohio Board of Pharmacy will review your complete application, all submitted documentation, and examination scores. Upon approval, your pharmacist license will be issued and viewable on the eLicense Portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Ohio pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Ohio have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Ohio. To work in Ohio, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Ohio?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Ohio
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Ohio, applicants must submit their application and required documentation through the Ohio eLicense Portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Ohio eLicense Portal (elicense.ohio.gov), click 'Register for an Account', and follow the prompts to create your professional licensing account before starting your application.
